CWE-89.title: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an SQL Command ('SQL Injection')
CWE-89.description: The software constructs all or part of an SQL command using externally-influenced input from an upstream component, but it does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes that input.
CWE-89.remediation: Use prepared statements with parameterized queries, or use an Object Relational Mapper (ORM).
CWE-89.risk: An attacker can manipulate the query structure to bypass authentication, read sensitive data, or modify database contents.

CWE-79.title: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')
CWE-79.description: The software does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes user-controllable input before it is placed in output that is used as a web page that is served to other users.
CWE-79.remediation: Encode output based on the context (HTML, JS, CSS) and use a Content Security Policy (CSP).
CWE-79.risk: Attackers can execute malicious scripts in the victim's browser, leading to session theft or site defacement.

CWE-78.title: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an OS Command ('OS Command Injection')
CWE-78.description: The software constructs all or part of an OS command using externally-influenced input, allowing attackers to execute arbitrary commands on the host OS.
CWE-78.remediation: Avoid calling OS commands directly. Use built-in library functions instead, or strictly validate input against a whitelist.
CWE-78.risk: Full system compromise. Attackers can gain complete control over the server.

CWE-347.title: Improper Verification of Cryptographic Signature
CWE-347.description: The software does not verify, or incorrectly verifies, the cryptographic signature for data which can lead to trust in forged or modified data.
CWE-347.remediation: Always verify signatures using strong, modern algorithms. Ensure the verification logic cannot be bypassed by 'none' algorithms.
CWE-347.risk: Authentication bypass and data integrity loss.

CWE-798.title: Use of Hard-coded Credentials
CWE-798.description: The software contains hard-coded credentials, such as a password or cryptographic key, which it uses for its own inbound authentication.
CWE-798.remediation: Store credentials in environment variables or secure vaults. Do not embed them in source code.
CWE-798.risk: Unauthorized access to external services or local accounts.
